platform: 苏州城市建设投资发展集团有限公司
company_name: 苏州城市建设投资发展集团有限公司
formal_event: Prospectus states that the company does not undertake government financing functions and that new debt after January 1 2015 is not local government debt.
continued_function: Formal no-government-financing language coexists with continuing evidence that the same issuer remains Suzhou's main urban infrastructure and public-utility investment construction and operation body, is described as responsible for project funding and debt repayment, and holds delegated-project accounts, fiscal special funds, and government-directed stakes in new-town and logistics projects.
ambiguity_note: The legal opinion states that the issuer has no PPP BT government purchase service project repurchase government-project advance-financing or land-reserve functions, so the case may also be read as formal fiscal substitution rather than continuing off-budget financing.
caveat: The current packet relies on issuer and legal-opinion disclosures from the same 2026 issuance page rather than an independent government or regulator exit-list document.
notes: New validated case added in the June 30 expansion; strongest high historical-capacity nominal-exit contrast against Ningbo substantive exit and Guangzhou fiscal substitution.

platform: 广州市城市建设投资集团有限公司
company_name: 广州市城市建设投资集团有限公司
formal_event: Issuer and rating disclosures state that since 2014 the company no longer participated in new government-project investment-financing and shifted to fiscal-funded entrusted project management.
continued_function: The company moved away from new government-project investment-financing toward budget-funded project management; old and current disclosures both state the post-2014 entrusted-management model and the 2018 announcement shows part of the 2014 MTN being recognized as government-repayment-responsibility debt and included in the fiscal budget for replacement.
ambiguity_note: The company still performs public infrastructure and project-management functions and holds large public-welfare assets so the label depends on distinguishing budgetary project management from off-budget government financing.
caveat: This is a fiscal-substitution case rather than an exit-list case; final confidence should be raised only after collecting the original Guangzhou 2008 reform document or additional 2014 issuance materials.
notes: Use as second human-validated pilot label and contrast case against Xi'an Hi-tech.

platform: 宁波开发投资集团有限公司
company_name: 宁波开发投资集团有限公司
formal_event: Issuer prospectus states that the company does not undertake government financing functions and that new debt after January 1 2015 is not local government debt; the same compliance section reports no PPP government investment fund BT repurchase land preparation government purchase service or advance financing for government projects.
continued_function: The no-government-financing statement is paired with unusually strong negative evidence on core platform functions: no PPP government investment fund BT project repurchase land-preparation business government purchase service or government-project advance financing. The case is therefore coded as substantive exit rather than nominal exit.
ambiguity_note: The current source is an issuer prospectus rather than a separately collected government exit-list document. A small receivable from a demolition office is disclosed as having an engineering background, so it should be monitored in later validation.
caveat: Confidence should rise only if an independent Ningbo or regulator source confirms the platform-list status.
notes: New validated case added in the June 28 expansion; useful as a high historical-capacity substantive-exit case.

platform: 杭州市城市建设投资集团有限公司
company_name: 杭州市城市建设投资集团有限公司
formal_event: Issuer disclosures state that Hangzhou City Investment and Qiantou Group were integrated into a new city-investment group and that Anju Group was created as a housing-security investment construction and operation platform.
continued_function: Local public investment and project functions did not disappear but were consolidated into an enlarged Hangzhou City Investment system through Qiantou Group equity transfer Qiantou debt succession and the creation of Anju Group; rating evidence shows continuing infrastructure public-utility代建 land-development and PPP functions.
ambiguity_note: Some代建 activity is described as management-fee based and not direct financing but the larger pattern is consolidation of public project and debt functions rather than disappearance of those functions.
caveat: This is best interpreted as functional consolidation within the functional-transfer family; confidence should be raised only after collecting the underlying Hangzhou SASAC reorganization approvals and Qiantou debt-succession announcements.
notes: Use as third human-validated pilot label to complete initial contrast among nominal exit substantive exit and functional transfer.

platform: 济南城市建设集团有限公司
company_name: 济南城市建设集团有限公司
formal_event: Prospectus states that the issuer does not undertake government financing functions and that new debt after January 1 2015 is not local government debt; the same compliance section reports that the statement was confirmed with the Jinan Finance Bureau.
continued_function: Formal no-government-financing language coexists with continuing engineering construction, land preparation, and delegated infrastructure-construction functions. The issuer discloses PPP and BT or entrusted-construction arrangements, a historical project placed under hidden-debt management and resolved, government receivables, land injected by public authorities, and current Jinan Start-up Area infrastructure projects operated through entrusted construction and settlement with public-sector counterparties.
ambiguity_note: Many continuing functions are presented as legal fiscal-settlement or marketized construction businesses, so the case is close to formal fiscal substitution rather than unreformed platform borrowing.
caveat: No independent regulator or local government platform-list exit document has been collected. The label relies on issuer prospectus compliance language and should remain medium confidence until a list-level exit source is found.
notes: New same-city second-platform validated case for Jinan; useful for checking whether city-level capacity predicts platform-level institutional fate consistently.

platform: 福州城市建设投资集团有限公司
company_name: 福州城市建设投资集团有限公司
formal_event: Prospectus states that the company does not undertake government financing functions and that new debt after January 1 2015 is not local government debt.
continued_function: Formal no-government-financing language coexists with a major reorganization of public-project finance. The issuer remains a leading Fuzhou infrastructure, shantytown-renovation, affordable-housing, municipal代建, and area-development platform, but the prospectus documents that after 2011 municipal project funding shifted from issuer financing with fiscal repayment to full fiscal appropriation and management-fee settlement. PPP projects, government-purchase-service projects that were later adjusted, fiscal subsidies, government receivables, and rating evidence of continuing fiscal support all point to functional transfer rather than substantive disappearance.
ambiguity_note: Because the same legal issuer continues to carry many public development tasks, a narrower reading would code the case as nominal exit. The functional-transfer label rests on the documented shift in project financing and settlement rules rather than on the disappearance of the function.
caveat: No independent platform-list exit document has been collected. Historical-capacity metrics for this added Fuzhou target still need to be merged into the candidate capacity file.
notes: New Fujian provincial-capital validated case; useful as a formal fiscal-substitution contrast to nominal-exit cases.

notes: New validated case added in the June 28 expansion; useful for high-capacity functional transfer.

platform: 西安高新控股有限公司
company_name: 西安高新控股有限公司
formal_event: Issuer disclosures state that the company exited the financing-platform list in 2012 and bank loans became ordinary issuer loans.
continued_function: Official exit is documented but the company continued to serve as an important infrastructure investment construction and entrusted-construction body for Xi'an High-tech Zone after exit; bond and rating materials describe BT or entrusted-construction arrangements government repurchase/project payments and continuing infrastructure financing pressure.
ambiguity_note: Some business modules were transferred after 2019 but the available evidence still shows the same issuer retaining core infrastructure construction and financing-related functions rather than a clean transfer of the whole quasi-fiscal role.
caveat: Exit event is supported by issuer disclosures across 2020 and 2026 rather than by a directly retrieved government exit-list document.

platform: 温州市名城建设投资集团有限公司
company_name: 温州市名城建设投资集团有限公司
formal_event: Issuer and guarantor disclosures state no government financing function after 2015; the issuer reports that it was outside the 2013 audit platform list and had not entered the banking regulator platform list at the issuer level.
continued_function: The borrower-level compliance language coexists with continuing city-development functions across the municipal construction group: entrusted redevelopment land preparation代建 affordable-housing work public-utility infrastructure investment and fiscal代建 receivables. This pattern is best coded as functional transfer inside a corporate group rather than disappearance of the public function.
ambiguity_note: The issuer itself says it had not entered the banking-regulator platform list, so the formal event is weaker than in Hengyang or Zhuzhou. The useful signal is group-level functional relocation after compliance disclosure.
caveat: Treat as a corporate-group functional-transfer case unless later sources show a direct list exit by the same legal entity.
notes: New validated case added in the June 28 expansion; helps separate issuer-level compliance from group-level public functions.

platform: 衡阳市城市建设投资有限公司
company_name: 衡阳市城市建设投资有限公司
formal_event: Prospectus reports that the issuer exited the local government financing platform enterprise list in September 2011, was brought back into platform management in March 2012 because it undertook affordable-housing construction, and was formally transferred out of CBIRC platform management on February 25 2020.
continued_function: This is a direct formal-exit case, but the same issuer continued entrusted construction, project financing and settlement with government, land preparation entrusted by planning authorities, infrastructure and affordable-housing construction, fiscal subsidies, and government support. The exit is therefore institutional in name but functionally incomplete.
ambiguity_note: The post-2020 model may involve legal service contracts and fiscal settlement rather than illegal off-budget borrowing, so the case is close to formal fiscal substitution.
caveat: Original 2020 regulator transfer-out documentation has not yet been collected outside the prospectus.
notes: New strongest validated case in the expansion because it has direct exit re-entry and transfer-out language.
